   436	function hasGovernanceAuditFilters(filters: GovernanceAuditReviewFilters): boolean {
   437	  const normalized = normalizeScopeContext(filters);
   438	  return Boolean(
   439	    filters.action
   440	    || filters.decision
   441	    || normalized.tenantId
   442	    || normalized.userId
   443	    || normalized.agentId
   444	    || normalized.sessionId
   445	    || normalized.sharedSpaceId
   446	  );
   447	}
   448	
   449	/**
   450	 * Build a reusable row predicate for scope filtering without re-normalizing each row scan.
   451	 *
   452	 * @param scope - Requested scope used for filtering.
   453	 * @param allowedSharedSpaceIds - Optional shared-space allowlist for the scope.
   454	 * @returns Predicate that returns `true` when a row remains visible.
   455	 */
   456	export function createScopeFilterPredicate<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(
   457	  scope: ScopeContext,
   458	  allowedSharedSpaceIds?: ReadonlySet<string>,
   459	): (row: T) => boolean {
   460	  const normalized = normalizeScopeContext(scope);
   461	  if (!isScopeEnforcementEnabled() && !hasScopeConstraints(normalized)) {
   462	    return () => true;
   463	  }
   464	  if (isScopeEnforcementEnabled() && !hasScopeConstraints(normalized)) {
   465	    // BUG-001 fix: Empty scope under enforcement means no access, not universal access.
   466	    return () => false;
   467	  }
   468	
   469	  return (row: T) => {
   470	    const rowSharedSpaceId = normalizeId(row.shared_space_id);
   471	    if (rowSharedSpaceId) {
   472	      if (normalized.sharedSpaceId && rowSharedSpaceId !== normalized.sharedSpaceId) {
   473	        return false;
   474	      }
   475	      if (allowedSharedSpaceIds && !allowedSharedSpaceIds.has(rowSharedSpaceId)) {
   476	        return false;
   477	      }
   478	    } else if (normalized.sharedSpaceId) {
   479	      return false;
   480	    }
   481	
   482	    // H8 FIX: When a row belongs to an allowed shared space, use membership as
   483	    // the access boundary — don't require exact actor/session match, which would
   484	    // block collaborator B from seeing collaborator A's shared memories.
   485	    if (rowSharedSpaceId && allowedSharedSpaceIds?.has(rowSharedSpaceId)) {
   486	      return matchesExactScope(row.tenant_id, normalized.tenantId);
   487	    }
   488	
   489	    return matchesExactScope(row.tenant_id, normalized.tenantId)
   490	      && matchesExactScope(row.user_id, normalized.userId)
   491	      && matchesExactScope(row.agent_id, normalized.agentId)
   492	      && matchesExactScope(row.session_id, normalized.sessionId);
   493	  };
   494	}
   495	
   496	/**
   497	 * Review governance audit rows and aggregate counts for a filtered governance window.
   498	 *
   499	 * @param database - Database connection that stores governance state.
   500	 * @param filters - Optional audit filters and row limit.
   501	 * @returns Review rows plus aggregate counts for the matching audit window.
   502	 */
   503	export function reviewGovernanceAudit(
   504	  database: Database.Database,
   505	  filters: GovernanceAuditReviewFilters = {},
   506	): GovernanceAuditReviewResult {
   507	  ensureGovernanceRuntime(database);
   508	  const allowUnscoped = filters.allowUnscoped === true;
   509	  // Security: audit enumeration requires explicit scope filters or admin override
   510	  if (!allowUnscoped && !hasGovernanceAuditFilters(filters)) {
   511	    console.warn('[scope-governance] Unscoped governance audit enumeration blocked; explicit filters or allowUnscoped=true required.');
   512	    return {
   513	      rows: [],
   514	      summary: {
   515	        totalMatching: 0,
   516	        returnedRows: 0,
   517	        byAction: {},
   518	        byDecision: {},
   519	        latestCreatedAt: null,
   520	      },
   521	    };
   522	  }
   523	  const { whereSql, params } = buildGovernanceAuditWhereClause(filters);
   524	  const limit = Number.isInteger(filters.limit) && (filters.limit ?? 0) > 0
   525	    ? Math.trunc(filters.limit as number)
   526	    : 50;
   527	
   528	  const rows = database.prepare(`
   529	    SELECT
   530	      id,
   531	      action,
   532	      decision,
   533	      memory_id,
   534	      logical_key,
   535	      tenant_id,
   536	      user_id,
   537	      agent_id,
   538	      session_id,
   539	      shared_space_id,
   540	      reason,
   541	      metadata,
   542	      created_at
   543	    FROM governance_audit
   544	    ${whereSql}
   545	    ORDER BY id DESC
   546	    LIMIT ?
   547	  `).all(...params, limit) as Array<{
   548	    id: number;
   549	    action: string;
   550	    decision: GovernanceAuditEntry['decision'];
   551	    memory_id: number | null;
   552	    logical_key: string | null;
   553	    tenant_id: string | null;
   554	    user_id: string | null;
   555	    agent_id: string | null;
   556	    session_id: string | null;
   557	    shared_space_id: string | null;
   558	    reason: string | null;
   559	    metadata: string | null;
   560	    created_at: string;
   561	  }>;
   562	
   563	  const summaryRow = database.prepare(`
   564	    SELECT
   565	      COUNT(*) AS total_matching,
   566	      MAX(created_at) AS latest_created_at
   567	    FROM governance_audit
   568	    ${whereSql}
   569	  `).get(...params) as {
   570	    total_matching: number;
   571	    latest_created_at: string | null;
   572	  };
   573	  const aggregateRows = database.prepare(`
   574	    SELECT
   575	      'action' AS aggregate_kind,
   576	      action AS aggregate_key,
   577	      COUNT(*) AS aggregate_count
   578	    FROM governance_audit
   579	    ${whereSql}
   580	    GROUP BY action
   581	    UNION ALL
   582	    SELECT
   583	      'decision' AS aggregate_kind,
   584	      decision AS aggregate_key,
   585	      COUNT(*) AS aggregate_count
   586	    FROM governance_audit
   587	    ${whereSql}
   588	    GROUP BY decision
   589	  `).all(...params, ...params) as Array<{
   590	    aggregate_kind: 'action' | 'decision';
   591	    aggregate_key: string;
   592	    aggregate_count: number;
   593	  }>;
   594	
   595	  const totalMatching = summaryRow.total_matching;
   596	  const byActionRows = aggregateRows.filter((row) => row.aggregate_kind === 'action');
   597	  const byDecisionRows = aggregateRows.filter((row) => row.aggregate_kind === 'decision');
   598	
   599	  return {
   600	    rows: rows.map((row) => ({
   601	      id: row.id,
   602	      action: row.action,
   603	      decision: row.decision,
   604	      memoryId: row.memory_id,
   605	      logicalKey: row.logical_key,
   606	      tenantId: row.tenant_id ?? undefined,
   607	      userId: row.user_id ?? undefined,
   608	      agentId: row.agent_id ?? undefined,
   609	      sessionId: row.session_id ?? undefined,
   610	      sharedSpaceId: row.shared_space_id ?? undefined,
   611	      reason: row.reason,
   612	      metadata: parseAuditMetadata(row.metadata),
   613	      createdAt: row.created_at,
   614	    })),
   615	    summary: {
   616	      totalMatching,
   617	      returnedRows: rows.length,
   618	      byAction: Object.fromEntries(byActionRows.map((row) => [row.aggregate_key, row.aggregate_count])),
   619	      byDecision: Object.fromEntries(byDecisionRows.map((row) => [row.aggregate_key as GovernanceAuditEntry['decision'], row.aggregate_count])),
   620	      latestCreatedAt: summaryRow.latest_created_at,
   621	    },
   622	  };
   623	}
   624	
   625	/**
   626	 * Benchmark scope filtering with a reusable predicate for rollout and safety checks.
   627	 *
   628	 * @param rows - Candidate rows that include governance scope columns.
   629	 * @param scope - Requested scope used for filtering.
   630	 * @param options - Optional iterations and shared-space allowlist.
   631	 * @returns Timing and match counts for the benchmark run.
   632	 */
   633	export function benchmarkScopeFilter<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(
   634	  rows: T[],
   635	  scope: ScopeContext,
   636	  options: ScopeFilterBenchmarkOptions = {},
   637	): ScopeFilterBenchmarkResult {
   638	  const iterations = Number.isInteger(options.iterations) && (options.iterations ?? 0) > 0
   639	    ? Math.trunc(options.iterations as number)
   640	    : 1;
   641	  const predicate = createScopeFilterPredicate(scope, options.allowedSharedSpaceIds);
   642	  let matchedRows = 0;
   643	  const startedAt = process.hrtime.bigint();
   644	
   645	  for (let iteration = 0; iteration < iterations; iteration += 1) {
   646	    matchedRows = 0;
   647	    for (const row of rows) {
   648	      if (predicate(row)) {
   649	        matchedRows += 1;
   650	      }
   651	    }
   652	  }
   653	
   654	  const elapsedMs = Number(process.hrtime.bigint() - startedAt) / 1_000_000;
   655	  return {
   656	    iterations,
   657	    totalRows: rows.length,
   658	    matchedRows,
   659	    filteredRows: rows.length - matchedRows,
   660	    elapsedMs,
   661	    averageMsPerIteration: elapsedMs / iterations,
   662	  };
   663	}
   664	
   665	/**
   666	 * Filter result rows to the tenant, actor, session, and shared-space scope in force.
   667	 *
   668	 * @param rows - Candidate rows that include governance scope columns.
   669	 * @param scope - Requested scope used for filtering.
   670	 * @param allowedSharedSpaceIds - Optional shared-space allowlist for the scope.
   671	 * @returns Rows that remain visible after governance filtering.
   672	 */
   673	export function filterRowsByScope<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(rows: T[], scope: ScopeContext, allowedSharedSpaceIds?: ReadonlySet<string>): T[] {
   674	  return rows.filter(createScopeFilterPredicate(scope, allowedSharedSpaceIds));
   675	}

   350	 * @param options - Optional cursor configuration (TTL).
   351	 * @returns Object with next page of results, next cursor (if more), or null if cursor is invalid/expired.
   352	 * @example
   353	 * ```ts
   354	 * const page = resolveCursor(cursor, 5);
   355	 * ```
   356	 */
   357	function resolveCursor(
   358	  cursor: string,
   359	  pageSize: number = DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE,
   360	  options?: CursorOptions,
   361	): { results: DisclosureResult[]; continuation: CursorInfo | null } | null {
   362	  const ttlMs = options?.ttlMs ?? DEFAULT_CURSOR_TTL_MS;
   363	  const payload = decodeCursor(cursor);
   364	  if (!payload) return null;
   365	  const storeKey = payload.cursorKey ?? payload.queryHash;

   367	  if (
   368	    typeof payload.scopeKey === 'string'
   369	    && payload.scopeKey.length > 0
   370	    && payload.scopeKey !== options?.scopeKey
   371	  ) {
   372	    return null;
   373	  }
   374	
   375	  pruneExpiredCursorEntries(ttlMs);
   376	
   377	  // Check expiry
   378	  const now = Date.now();
   379	  if (now - payload.timestamp > ttlMs) {
   380	    // Expired cursor — clean up stored data
   381	    cursorStore.delete(storeKey);
   382	    return null;
   383	  }
   384	
   385	  // Retrieve stored result set
   386	  const stored = cursorStore.get(storeKey);
   387	  if (!stored) return null;
   388	
   389	  // Extract the requested page
   390	  const start = payload.offset;

// Phase 004 T011: Trigger phrase filter — suppresses path fragments, short tokens, and shingle subsets
const TRIGGER_ALLOW_LIST = new Set(['rag', 'bm25', 'mcp', 'adr', 'jwt', 'api', 'cli', 'llm', 'ai']);
function normalizeWorkflowTriggerKey(value) {
    return value.toLowerCase().replace(/[-_]+/g, ' ').replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();
}
function filterTriggerPhrases(phrases, manualPhraseKeys = new Set()) {
    // Stage 1: Remove entries containing path separators (forward/backslash, multi-word path segments)
    let filtered = phrases.filter(p => {
        const trimmed = p.trim();
        const comparisonKey = normalizeWorkflowTriggerKey(trimmed);
        if (trimmed.includes('/') || trimmed.includes('\\'))
            return false;
        if (manualPhraseKeys.has(comparisonKey)) {
            return true;
        }
        if (/^\d{1,3}\s/.test(trimmed)) {
            return false;
        }
        return true;
    });
    // Stage 2: Remove entries where every word is under 3 characters (unless in allow-list)
    filtered = filtered.filter(p => {
        const comparisonKey = normalizeWorkflowTriggerKey(p);
        if (manualPhraseKeys.has(comparisonKey)) {
            return true;
        }
        const words = p.trim().split(/\s+/);
        if (words.length === 1 && words[0].length < 3 && !TRIGGER_ALLOW_LIST.has(words[0].toLowerCase())) {
            return false;
        }
        // Multi-word: keep if at least one word >= 3 chars or any word is in allow-list
        if (words.every(w => w.length < 3) && !words.some(w => TRIGGER_ALLOW_LIST.has(w.toLowerCase()))) {
            return false;
        }
        return true;
    });
    // Stage 3: Remove n-gram shingle phrases that are substrings of longer retained phrases
    const lowerPhrases = filtered.map((p) => p.toLowerCase().replace(/[-_]+/g, ' ').replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim());
    filtered = filtered.filter((p, idx) => {
        const lower = lowerPhrases[idx];
        if (manualPhraseKeys.has(lower)) {
            return true;
        }
        if ((0, trigger_phrase_sanitizer_1.isAllowlistedShortProductName)(p)) {
            return true;
        }
        // Check if this phrase is a substring of any other (longer) phrase
        for (let j = 0; j < lowerPhrases.length; j++) {
            if (j !== idx && ((lowerPhrases[j] === lower && j < idx)
                || (lowerPhrases[j].length > lower.length && lowerPhrases[j].includes(lower)))) {
                return false;
            }
        }
        return true;
    });
    return filtered;
}
/**
 * Insert content after YAML frontmatter, preserving frontmatter at byte 0.
 * Frontmatter is a block delimited by `---\n` at position 0 and a closing `---\n`.
 * If no frontmatter is found, prepends the content (original behavior).
 */
function insertAfterFrontmatter(content, insertion) {
    if (!content.startsWith('---')) {
        return insertion + content;
    }
    // Find the closing --- (skip the opening ---)
    const closingIdx = content.indexOf('\n---', 3);
    if (closingIdx === -1) {
        return insertion + content;
    }
    // Find the end of the closing --- line
    const afterClosing = content.indexOf('\n', closingIdx + 4);
    const insertionPoint = afterClosing === -1 ? content.length : afterClosing + 1;
    return content.slice(0, insertionPoint) + insertion + content.slice(insertionPoint);
}

// Feature catalog: Prediction-error save arbitration


// Create local path validator
const validateFilePathLocal = createFilePathValidator(ALLOWED_BASE_PATHS, validateFilePath);
const MANUAL_FALLBACK_SOURCE_CLASSIFICATION = 'manual-fallback' as const;

interface PreparedParsedMemory {
  parsed: ReturnType<typeof memoryParser.parseMemoryFile>;
  validation: ReturnType<typeof memoryParser.validateParsedMemory>;
  qualityLoopResult: QualityLoopResult;
  sufficiencyResult: MemorySufficiencyResult;
  templateContract: MemoryTemplateContractResult;
  specDocHealth: SpecDocHealthResult | null;
  finalizedFileContent: string | null;
  sourceClassification: 'template-generated' | typeof MANUAL_FALLBACK_SOURCE_CLASSIFICATION;
}

type ParsedMemoryWithIndexHints = ReturnType<typeof memoryParser.parseMemoryFile> & {
  _skipIndex?: boolean;
  _vRuleIndexBlockIds?: string[];
};

const STANDARD_MEMORY_TEMPLATE_MARKERS = [
  '## continue session',
  '## recovery hints',
  '<!-- memory metadata -->',
];

class VRuleUnavailableError extends Error {
  constructor(message: string) {
    super(message);
    this.name = 'VRuleUnavailableError';
  }
}

function isVRuleUnavailableResult(value: unknown): value is {
  passed: false;
  status: 'error' | 'warning';
  message: string;
  _unavailable: true;
} {
  return typeof value === 'object'
    && value !== null
    && 'passed' in value
    && (value as { passed?: unknown }).passed === false
    && 'status' in value
    && typeof (value as { status?: unknown }).status === 'string'
    && 'message' in value
    && typeof (value as { message?: unknown }).message === 'string';
}

function classifyMemorySaveSource(
  content: string,
): 'template-generated' | typeof MANUAL_FALLBACK_SOURCE_CLASSIFICATION {
  const normalizedContent = content.toLowerCase();
  const hasAnyStandardMarker = STANDARD_MEMORY_TEMPLATE_MARKERS.some((marker) => normalizedContent.includes(marker));
  return hasAnyStandardMarker ? 'template-generated' : MANUAL_FALLBACK_SOURCE_CLASSIFICATION;
}

function shouldBypassTemplateContract(
  sourceClassification: PreparedParsedMemory['sourceClassification'],
  sufficiencyResult: MemorySufficiencyResult,
  templateContract: MemoryTemplateContractResult,
): boolean {
  return sourceClassification === MANUAL_FALLBACK_SOURCE_CLASSIFICATION
    && sufficiencyResult.pass
    && sufficiencyResult.evidenceCounts.primary === 0
    && sufficiencyResult.evidenceCounts.support >= 3
    && sufficiencyResult.evidenceCounts.anchors >= 1
    && !templateContract.valid;
}

function buildQualityLoopMetadata(
  parsed: ReturnType<typeof memoryParser.parseMemoryFile>,
  database: ReturnType<typeof requireDb>,
): Record<string, unknown> {
  return {
    title: parsed.title ?? '',
    triggerPhrases: parsed.triggerPhrases,
    specFolder: parsed.specFolder,
    contextType: parsed.contextType,
    importanceTier: parsed.importanceTier,
    causalLinks: parsed.causalLinks,
    filePath: parsed.filePath,
    lastModified: parsed.lastModified,
    resolveReference: (reference: string) => resolveMemoryReference(database, reference) !== null,
  };
}

function prepareParsedMemoryForIndexing(
  parsed: ReturnType<typeof memoryParser.parseMemoryFile>,
  database: ReturnType<typeof requireDb>,
  options: {
    emitEvalMetrics?: boolean;
  } = {},
): PreparedParsedMemory {
  const validation = memoryParser.validateParsedMemory(parsed);
  if (validation.warnings && validation.warnings.length > 0) {
    console.warn(`[memory] Warning for ${path.basename(parsed.filePath)}:`);
    validation.warnings.forEach((warning: string) => console.warn(`[memory]   - ${warning}`));
  }

  // O2-5/O2-12: Run V-rule validation (previously only in workflow path)
  const vRuleResult = validateMemoryQualityContent(parsed.content, { filePath: parsed.filePath });
  if (isVRuleUnavailableResult(vRuleResult) && vRuleResult.status === 'error') {
    throw new VRuleUnavailableError(vRuleResult.message);
  }
  if (vRuleResult && '_unavailable' in vRuleResult) {
    validation.warnings.push('V-rule validator module unavailable — quality gate bypassed. Save proceeds without V-rule enforcement.');
  }
  if (vRuleResult && !isVRuleUnavailableResult(vRuleResult) && !vRuleResult.valid) {
    const vRuleDisposition = determineValidationDisposition(
      vRuleResult.failedRules,
      parsed.memoryTypeSource || null,
    );
    if (vRuleDisposition && vRuleDisposition.disposition === 'abort_write') {
      const failedRuleIds = vRuleDisposition.blockingRuleIds;
      console.error(`[memory-save] V-rule hard block for ${path.basename(parsed.filePath)}: ${failedRuleIds.join(', ')}`);
      // Return early with a rejected quality loop result so callers see the block
      const rejectScore = { total: 0, breakdown: { triggers: 0, anchors: 0, budget: 0, coherence: 0 }, issues: [`V-rule hard block: ${failedRuleIds.join(', ')}`] };
      return {
        parsed,
        validation,
        qualityLoopResult: {
          passed: false,
          score: rejectScore,
          attempts: 0,
          fixes: [],
          rejected: true,
          rejectionReason: `V-rule hard block: ${failedRuleIds.join(', ')}`,
        },
        sufficiencyResult: {
          pass: false,
          rejectionCode: MEMORY_SUFFICIENCY_REJECTION_CODE,
          score: 0,
          reasons: [`V-rule hard block: ${failedRuleIds.join(', ')}`],
          evidenceCounts: { primary: 0, support: 0, total: 0, semanticChars: 0, uniqueWords: 0, anchors: 0, triggerPhrases: 0 },
        },
        templateContract: { valid: false, violations: [], missingAnchors: [], unexpectedTemplateArtifacts: [] } as MemoryTemplateContractResult,
        specDocHealth: null,
        finalizedFileContent: null,
        sourceClassification: 'template-generated',
      };
    }
    if (vRuleDisposition && vRuleDisposition.disposition === 'write_skip_index') {
      console.warn(`[memory-save] V-rule index block for ${path.basename(parsed.filePath)}: ${vRuleDisposition.indexBlockingRuleIds.join(', ')}`);
      validation.warnings.push(`V-rule index block: ${vRuleDisposition.indexBlockingRuleIds.join(', ')}`);
      // F07-002: Flag to skip indexing for write_skip_index disposition
      const parsedWithIndexHints = parsed as ParsedMemoryWithIndexHints;
      parsedWithIndexHints._skipIndex = true;
      parsedWithIndexHints._vRuleIndexBlockIds = vRuleDisposition.indexBlockingRuleIds;
    }
  }

I’ve now verified the core asymmetry: MemPalace centralizes everything into one local palace path and one collection, then relies on wing metadata for separation, while Public has explicit governed scope fields plus shared-space membership and conflict handling. I’m pulling one last round of exact lines for the MemPalace mine path and Public save lock, then I can synthesize the iteration cleanly.

   232	let workflowRunQueue = Promise.resolve();
   233	/** Filesystem lock directory for cross-process serialization. */
   234	const WORKFLOW_MODULE_DIR = __dirname;

   236	const WORKFLOW_LOCK_OWNER_PATH = path.join(WORKFLOW_LOCK_DIR, 'owner.json');
   237	const LEGACY_LOCK_STALE_MS = 5_000;
   238	function writeWorkflowLockOwner() {
   239	    const owner = {
   240	        pid: process.pid,
   241	        acquiredAt: new Date().toISOString(),
   242	    };
   243	    fsSync.writeFileSync(WORKFLOW_LOCK_OWNER_PATH, JSON.stringify(owner, null, 2), 'utf8');

   245	function isProcessAlive(pid) {
   246	    if (!Number.isInteger(pid) || pid <= 0) {
   247	        return false;
   248	    }
   249	    try {
   250	        process.kill(pid, 0);
   251	        return true;
   252	    }
   253	    catch (err) {
   254	        const code = err?.code;
   255	        return code !== 'ESRCH';
   256	    }
   257	}
   258	function clearWorkflowLockDir() {
   259	    try {
   260	        fsSync.rmSync(WORKFLOW_LOCK_DIR, { recursive: true, force: true });
   261	    }
   262	    catch (_err) {
   263	        // Best-effort stale lock cleanup.

   265	}
   266	function shouldClearStaleWorkflowLock() {
   267	    try {
   268	        const ownerRaw = fsSync.existsSync(WORKFLOW_LOCK_OWNER_PATH)
   269	            ? fsSync.readFileSync(WORKFLOW_LOCK_OWNER_PATH, 'utf8')
   270	            : null;
   271	        if (ownerRaw) {
   272	            const owner = JSON.parse(ownerRaw);
   273	            return !isProcessAlive(typeof owner.pid === 'number' ? owner.pid : NaN);
   274	        }
   275	        const lockStats = fsSync.statSync(WORKFLOW_LOCK_DIR);
   276	        return (Date.now() - lockStats.mtimeMs) >= LEGACY_LOCK_STALE_MS;
   277	    }
   278	    catch (_err) {
   279	        return true;
   280	    }
   281	}
   282	/**
   283	 * Acquire the filesystem lock via atomic mkdir.
   284	 * Uses exponential backoff; gives up after ~30 s total wait.
   285	 */
   286	async function acquireFilesystemLock() {
   287	    const MAX_TOTAL_MS = 30_000;
   288	    let waited = 0;
   289	    let delay = 100;
   290	    while (waited < MAX_TOTAL_MS) {
   291	        try {
   292	            fsSync.mkdirSync(WORKFLOW_LOCK_DIR, { recursive: false });
   293	            writeWorkflowLockOwner();
   294	            return true; // lock acquired
   295	        }
   296	        catch (err) {
   297	            if (err.code !== 'EEXIST') {
   298	                // Unexpected error (permissions, etc.) -- skip fs lock
   299	                return false;
   300	            }
   301	            if (shouldClearStaleWorkflowLock()) {
   302	                clearWorkflowLockDir();
   303	                continue;
   304	            }
   305	            // Lock held by another process -- wait with backoff
   306	            await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, delay));
   307	            waited += delay;
   308	            delay = Math.min(delay * 2, 4_000);
   309	        }
   310	    }
   311	    // Timed out -- proceed without fs lock (fallback to in-process queue)
   312	    console.warn('[workflow] Filesystem lock acquisition timed out after 30 s; proceeding without fs lock.');
   313	    return false;
   314	}
   315	function releaseFilesystemLock() {

   323	async function withWorkflowRunLock(operation) {

   341	    }
   342	}

   818	    // T028/T029: complete async reconsolidation planning before chunking or

   823	      parsed,

   826	      embedding,
   827	      scope,

   833	      const chunkedInsertTracker = createChunkedInsertTracker();
   834	      const chunkedResult = await indexChunkedMemoryFile(filePath, parsed, {
   835	        force,
   836	        applyPostInsertMetadata: (db, memoryId, fields) => {
   837	          applyPostInsertMetadata(db, memoryId, fields);
   838	          trackChunkedInsert(chunkedInsertTracker, memoryId, fields as Record<string, unknown>);
   839	        },
   840	      });

   842	      if (
   843	        peResult.supersededId != null
   844	        && (chunkedResult.status === 'indexed' || chunkedResult.status === 'updated')

     1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2	"""
     3	MemPalace — Give your AI a memory. No API key required.
     4	
     5	Two ways to ingest:
     6	  Projects:      mempalace mine ~/projects/my_app          (code, docs, notes)
     7	  Conversations: mempalace mine ~/chats/ --mode convos     (Claude, ChatGPT, Slack)
     8	
     9	Same palace. Same search. Different ingest strategies.
    10	
    11	Commands:
    12	    mempalace init <dir>                  Detect rooms from folder structure
    13	    mempalace split <dir>                 Split concatenated mega-files into per-session files
    14	    mempalace mine <dir>                  Mine project files (default)
    15	    mempalace mine <dir> --mode convos    Mine conversation exports
    16	    mempalace search "query"              Find anything, exact words
    17	    mempalace mcp                         Show MCP setup command
    18	    mempalace wake-up                     Show L0 + L1 wake-up context
    19	    mempalace wake-up --wing my_app       Wake-up for a specific project
    20	    mempalace status                      Show what's been filed
    21	
    22	Examples:
    23	    mempalace init ~/projects/my_app
    24	    mempalace mine ~/projects/my_app
    25	    mempalace mine ~/chats/claude-sessions --mode convos
    26	    mempalace search "why did we switch to GraphQL"
    27	    mempalace search "pricing discussion" --wing my_app --room costs
    28	"""
    29	
    30	import os
    31	import sys
    32	import shlex
    33	import argparse
    34	from pathlib import Path
    35	
    36	from .config import MempalaceConfig

    38	
    39	def cmd_init(args):
    40	    import json

    43	    from .room_detector_local import detect_rooms_local

    46	    print(f"\n  Scanning for entities in: {args.dir}")
    47	    files = scan_for_detection(args.dir)
    48	    if files:
    49	        print(f"  Reading {len(files)} files...")
    50	        detected = detect_entities(files)
    51	        total = len(detected["people"]) + len(detected["projects"]) + len(detected["uncertain"])
    52	        if total > 0:
    53	            confirmed = confirm_entities(detected, yes=getattr(args, "yes", False))
    54	            # Save confirmed entities to <project>/entities.json for the miner
    55	            if confirmed["people"] or confirmed["projects"]:
    56	                entities_path = Path(args.dir).expanduser().resolve() / "entities.json"
    57	                with open(entities_path, "w") as f:
    58	                    json.dump(confirmed, f, indent=2)
    59	                print(f"  Entities saved: {entities_path}")
    60	        else:
    61	            print("  No entities detected — proceeding with directory-based rooms.")
    62	
    63	    # Pass 2: detect rooms from folder structure
    64	    detect_rooms_local(project_dir=args.dir, yes=getattr(args, "yes", False))
    65	    MempalaceConfig().init()
    66	
    67	
    68	def cmd_mine(args):
    69	    palace_path = os.path.expanduser(args.palace) if args.palace else MempalaceConfig().palace_path
    70	    include_ignored = []
    71	    for raw in args.include_ignored or []:
    72	        include_ignored.extend(part.strip() for part in raw.split(",") if part.strip())

   232	def mine_convos(
   233	    convo_dir: str,

   241	    """Mine a directory of conversation files into the palace.
   242	
   243	    extract_mode:

   246	    """

   266	
   267	    collection = get_collection(palace_path) if not dry_run else None

   270	    files_skipped = 0

   279	            continue

   292	            from .general_extractor import extract_memories
   293

   299	        if not chunks:

[project]
name = "mempalace"
version = "3.1.0"
description = "Give your AI a memory — mine projects and conversations into a searchable palace. No API key required."
readme = "README.md"
requires-python = ">=3.9"
license = "MIT"
authors = [
    {name = "milla-jovovich"},
]
keywords = [
    "ai", "memory", "llm", "rag", "chromadb", "mcp",
    "vector-database", "claude", "chatgpt", "embeddings",
]
classifiers = [
    "Development Status :: 4 - Beta",
    "Environment :: Console",
    "Intended Audience :: Developers",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.9",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.10",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.11",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.12",
    "Topic :: Scientific/Engineering :: Artificial Intelligence",
    "Topic :: Utilities",
]
dependencies = [
    "chromadb>=0.5.0,<0.7",
    "pyyaml>=6.0,<7",
]

[project.urls]
Homepage = "https://github.com/milla-jovovich/mempalace"
Repository = "https://github.com/milla-jovovich/mempalace"
"Bug Tracker" = "https://github.com/milla-jovovich/mempalace/issues"

[project.scripts]
mempalace = "mempalace:main"

[project.optional-dependencies]
dev = ["pytest>=7.0", "pytest-cov>=4.0", "ruff>=0.4.0", "psutil>=5.9"]
spellcheck = ["autocorrect>=2.0"]

[dependency-groups]
dev = ["pytest>=7.0", "pytest-cov>=4.0", "ruff>=0.4.0", "psutil>=5.9"]

[build-system]
requires = ["hatchling"]
build-backend = "hatchling.build"

[tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel]
packages = ["mempalace"]

[tool.ruff]
line-length = 100
target-version = "py39"
extend-exclude = ["benchmarks"]

[tool.ruff.lint]
select = ["E", "F", "W", "C901"]
ignore = ["E501"]

[tool.ruff.lint.mccabe]
max-complexity = 25

[tool.ruff.format]
quote-style = "double"

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
testpaths = ["tests"]
pythonpath = ["."]
addopts = "-m 'not benchmark and not slow and not stress'"
markers = [
    "benchmark: scale/performance benchmark tests",
    "slow: tests that take more than 30 seconds",
    "stress: destructive scale tests (100K+ drawers)",
]

[tool.coverage.run]
source = ["mempalace"]

[tool.coverage.report]
fail_under = 85
show_missing = true
exclude_lines = [
    "if __name__",
    "pragma: no cover",
]

# Iteration 025: MULTI-AGENT MEMORY SAFETY

## Focus
MULTI-AGENT MEMORY SAFETY: How does this system handle concurrent agent access, merge conflicts, project isolation, and shared vs private memory scoping?

## Findings
### Finding 1: Drawer writes are idempotent, not collaborative
- **Source**: [mcp_server.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/mcp_server.py), [miner.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/miner.py), [convo_miner.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/convo_miner.py)
- **What it does**: MemPalace writes into one Chroma collection and relies on deterministic drawer IDs plus `upsert`/`add` behavior to avoid exact duplicate inserts. It keeps a JSONL write-ahead log, but there is no application-level lock, merge registry, or logical-key conflict policy for two agents writing different content into the same conceptual area.
- **Why it matters**: This is workable for single-user local capture, but it is not a true multi-agent merge-safety model. Concurrent agents can still create parallel truths or race through backend semantics with no explicit reconciliation surface.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 2: Project isolation is taxonomy-level, not storage-level
- **Source**: [config.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/config.py), [cli.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/cli.py), [miner.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/miner.py), [convo_miner.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/convo_miner.py)
- **What it does**: Both project mining and conversation mining target the same default palace path and the same `mempalace_drawers` collection. “Isolation” comes from `wing` naming and optional `wing`/`room` filters at query time, not from separate databases, tenants, or per-project ACLs.
- **Why it matters**: MemPalace is optimized for one shared local corpus with navigable partitions, not for hard project separation. That is useful taxonomy, but weak safety if we care about preventing bleed between projects or collaborators.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 3: “Private” agent memory is a naming convention, not an authorization boundary
- **Source**: [mcp_server.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/mcp_server.py)
- **What it does**: Agent diary entries are stored under `wing_<agent>/diary`, and reads filter on that wing. But the MCP surface exposes global listing and search tools without actor-scoped auth parameters, so diary separation is organizational rather than enforced private/public scoping.
- **Why it matters**: For multi-agent safety, “each agent has its own diary wing” sounds stronger than it is. Any caller with palace access is still operating against one shared server and one shared corpus.
- **Recommendation**: reject
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 4: The temporal KG is concurrency-tolerant locally, but globally shared logically
- **Source**: [knowledge_graph.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/knowledge_graph.py), [mcp_server.py](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-hybrid-rag-fusion-upgrade/001-research-hybrid-rag-fusion-systems/005-mempalace/external/mempalace/mcp_server.py)
- **What it does**: The KG uses SQLite with WAL mode and `check_same_thread=False`, and it deduplicates currently-valid identical triples before insert. That helps local write tolerance, but facts themselves carry no tenant, user, agent, session, or shared/private ownership fields, so invalidation mutates a global truth layer for the whole palace.
- **Why it matters**: The temporal fact model is still interesting, but copied as-is it would let one actor end or replace facts for everyone sharing that store.
- **Recommendation**: prototype later
- **Impact**: high

### Finding 5: Public already has the stronger multi-agent safety model
- **Source**: [scope-governance.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/governance/scope-governance.ts), [shared-spaces.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/collab/shared-spaces.ts), [shared-memory.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/shared-memory.ts), [memory-save.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-save.ts), [workflow.js](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/dist/core/workflow.js), [progressive-disclosure.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/progressive-disclosure.ts), [session-manager.ts](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/session/session-manager.ts)
- **What it does**: Public validates governed ingest with `tenantId`/`sessionId`/`userId` or `agentId` plus provenance, enforces deny-by-default shared-space membership and role checks, records shared conflicts with explicit strategies, serializes save work with spec-folder locks plus transactions, and binds cursors/session reuse to scope identity.
- **Why it matters**: The safe direction is not to import MemPalace’s storage model wholesale. The useful imports are taxonomy and temporal ideas, but only on top of Public’s existing scope, conflict, and lock primitives.
- **Recommendation**: adopt now
- **Impact**: high

## Assessment
- New information ratio: 0.46

## Recommended Next Focus
1. Verify whether MemPalace’s test suite exercises simultaneous writers at all, or whether concurrency safety is entirely implicit in Chroma and SQLite behavior.
2. Design a Public-only taxonomy and diary layer that inherits existing `tenantId` / `userId` / `agentId` / `sharedSpaceId` boundaries instead of replacing them.
3. If Public gets a temporal fact plane, require ownership, shared-space awareness, and conflict semantics from day one so invalidation cannot become a global unscoped mutation.
